docs.title: Research Compendium
docs.text:
Welcome to my centralized knowledge base. This repository serves as a living documentation space for my applied research, technical workflows, and data architecture implementations.

As a researcher transitioning into doctoral studies in computer science, I utilize this space to meticulously catalog standard operating procedures and algorithmic designs. The primary focus areas of this compendium include:

  • Machine Learning & Deep Learning: Model optimization, experiment tracking, and image processing methodologies.
  • Natural Language Processing & LLMs: Text processing pipelines, fine-tuning strategies, and applied sentiment analysis.
  • Data Infrastructure: Scalable data management techniques and engineering best practices.
  • Version Control & Workflows: Protocols for maintaining clean, reproducible research environments.

Please use the top navigation menu to browse specific guides, code snippets, and research notes.

docs.title: Executing the Git Reset in VS Code
docs.text: Visual Studio Code's graphical interface does not support creating "orphan branches." Therefore, the most efficient method is to execute the exact same commands using VS Code's Integrated Terminal.

docs.title: Step 1: Open the Integrated Terminal
docs.text:
1. Open your repository in VS Code.
  2. From the top menu, select Terminal > New Terminal (or press `⌃ + `` on your keyboard).
  3. Ensure the terminal dropdown menu on the right indicates zsh or bash.

docs.title: Step 2: Execute the Commands
docs.text:
Copy and paste the sequence directly into the VS Code terminal. Press Enter after each line.

git checkout --orphan temp_branch
git add -A
git commit -m "Initial commit"
git branch -D main
git branch -m main
git push -f origin main

Note: This will completely overwrite the remote repository history, resulting in a single, clean commit.

docs.title: Step 3: Deploy the Updates
docs.text:
Because we previously set up GitHub Actions, deploying these changes is fully automated. You only need to push your local files to GitHub.

Run these commands in your VS Code terminal:

git add .
git commit -m "Apply minimalist theme and add Git Reset guide"
git push
